user.title: GivEnergy Local
user.description: Connect to your GivEnergy inverter via local Modbus TCP.

data.passive: Passive mode (listen only — another client drives refreshes)
data.battery_data_only: Battery data only (suppress controls and system sensors)

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure GivEnergy Local
reconfigure.description: Update the connection or refresh settings for this inverter. The integration reloads automatically when you save.

data.passive: Passive mode (listen only — another client drives refreshes)

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the inverter. Check the IP address and port.
error.wrong_inverter: The inverter at that address has a different serial number from the one originally configured. Remove and re-add the integration if you want to switch inverters.

init.title: GivEnergy Local options
init.description: Battery-data-only mode suppresses all control entities and inverter-level system sensors (PV, grid, load, derived consumption), leaving only the battery pack, HV stack, AIO module, and diagnostic data. Use this when this unit is controlled by a Gateway in a parallel group, where its per-unit controls and derived figures are misleading.

data.battery_data_only: Battery data only (suppress controls and system sensors)
data.warn_clock_drift: Warn when the inverter clock drifts from Home Assistant

data_description.expose_per_cell: Create an entity for every individual cell voltage and temperature on each battery pack, AIO module, and HV module. This can be a large number of entities (a six-module HV stack is roughly 288), so it is off by default on new installs — the per-module min/max/delta roll-ups are always available regardless. Existing installations keep their per-cell entities unless you turn this off.

experimental.name: Experimental features
experimental.description: Leave these off unless a maintainer has asked you to enable them.

data_description.splice_reject_heal: Allows the battery splice-guard to recover from legitimate LiFePO4 charge-knee surges (voltage/capacity class, multi-poll streak gate) rather than keeping the battery permanently offline. Enable only if you see repeated battery disconnects near full charge and a maintainer has confirmed this is the right path.

init.title: A known GivEnergy device stopped responding
init.description:
These device(s) were part of your system last time but did not respond on the latest connection: {devices}.

The integration has NOT removed them — it is still serving the rest of your system, and the affected entities show as unavailable. This is usually a transient comms issue (for example a slow battery BMS); the previous layout is kept and retried, so it normally clears on its own.

If the device has come back, or if you have genuinely removed it, click Fix to clear the cached layout and run a fresh hardware detection. Otherwise you can dismiss this — the integration picks the device back up automatically on its next successful detection.

system_time_drift.title: GivEnergy inverter clock has drifted (~{drift_minutes} min)

init.title: Inverter clock is out by about {drift_minutes} minutes
init.description:
The inverter's clock reads {system_time} but Home Assistant's time is {ha_time} — a drift of about {drift_minutes} minutes. The inverter runs its charge/discharge time-of-use slots from its own clock, so a drift this large can make those slots (and Predbat scheduling) act at the wrong time.

Click **Submit** to set the inverter clock to Home Assistant's current time.

If you deliberately keep your inverter in a different time zone (for example UTC), turn this warning off instead: open the integration's **Configure** dialog and untick *Warn when the inverter clock drifts from Home Assistant*.

system_time_drift_ems.title: GivEnergy EMS controller clock has drifted (~{drift_minutes} min)
system_time_drift_ems.description:
The EMS controller's clock reads {system_time} but Home Assistant's time is {ha_time} — a drift of about {drift_minutes} minutes. The controller runs its charge and export slots from its own clock, so a drift this large can make those slots (and Predbat scheduling) act at the wrong time.

Unlike a directly-connected inverter, an EMS controller's clock cannot be set locally — correct it from the GivEnergy app or portal. The controller normally re-syncs its clock from the cloud, so this often clears on its own.

If you deliberately run your system in a different time zone (for example UTC), turn this warning off instead: open the integration's **Configure** dialog and untick *Warn when the inverter clock drifts from Home Assistant*.

plant_topology_changed.title: GivEnergy plant topology has changed
plant_topology_changed.description:
The hardware layout of your GivEnergy system has changed since the last start — typically a battery was added or removed, or the inverter device type changed.

The integration has already accepted the new topology and reloaded itself; this notice is purely advisory and can be dismissed.

ems_entity_ids_outdated.title: GivEnergy EMS entity IDs need recreating
ems_entity_ids_outdated.description:
This update gave your EMS controller its own device identity, so its entities are now named `givenergy_ems_…` instead of `givenergy_inverter_…`. Their recorded history is preserved.

To move your existing EMS entities onto the new names: open the **EMS device**, use the top-right **⋮ menu → Recreate entity IDs**.

Any automations or dashboard cards that referenced the old `givenergy_inverter_…` EMS entities will need a one-line update. This notice clears itself once the IDs are recreated.

redetect_plant.name: Re-detect plant topology
redetect_plant.description: Clears the cached plant topology and reloads the integration, forcing a full hardware detection sweep on the next connect.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The GivEnergy inverter whose plant topology should be re-detected.

expose_recommended_entities.name: Expose recommended entities to voice assistants
expose_recommended_entities.description: One-shot service that exposes an opinionated headline set of entities (battery SOC, PV/grid/load power, today's and lifetime energy totals, inverter status) to one or more voice/LLM assistants. Idempotent and never un-exposes anything.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The GivEnergy inverter whose entities should be exposed.

assistants.name: Assistants
assistants.description: Which assistant platforms to expose entities to. Defaults to `conversation`.

capture_frames.name: Capture debug frames
capture_frames.description: Records raw Modbus wire frames from the inverter for a set duration and saves them as a redacted text file with an environment header. A persistent notification links to a page to inspect, download, or report the capture.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The GivEnergy inverter to capture from. If omitted, the first connected inverter is used.

duration.name: Duration (seconds)
duration.description: How long to capture frames for. Defaults to 60 seconds.
